content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

Adds new term **GO:7770059 alpha helical protein insertion into mitochondrial outer membrane** as requested in #31711 (item 2 of the mitochondrial import pathway reorganization).

## Term details

```
id: GO:7770059
name: alpha helical protein insertion into mitochondrial outer membrane
namespace: biological_process
def: "The import of alpha helical and tail-anchored proteins from the cytosol and their insertion into the mitochondrial outer membrane." [PMID:40782022]
comment: In yeast, the insertase is the mitochondrial import (MIM) complex, composed of Mim1 and Mim2. In higher eukaryotes, the functional counterpart MTCH performs a similar role despite lacking sequence and structural homology, reflecting convergent evolution.
synonym: "MIM pathway" NARROW []
synonym: "MTCH pathway" NARROW []
is_a: GO:7770058 ! mitochondrial protein import pathway
```

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue analyzed, intent clear
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: Ontology validates before changes
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Relevant terms consulted (GO:0045040, GO:7770058, GO:0051204)
- [x] EDITS: Checkout/checkin procedure followed
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: is_a to GO:7770058 (mitochondrial protein import pathway) — consistent with the reorganization structure in #31711
- [x] METADATA: namespace, definition with PMID, synonyms with scopes, term_tracker_item, created_by, creation_date all present
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: `make travis_build` passes (all 20 SPARQL checks pass, ELK reasoning succeeds)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED

---

content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

- Adds new term **GO:7770059** "alpha helical protein insertion into mitochondrial outer membrane" (item 2 from #31711)
- **is_a**: GO:0045040 (protein insertion into mitochondrial outer membrane), GO:7770058 (mitochondrial protein import pathway)
- **Definition**: "The import of alpha helical and tail-anchored proteins from the cytosol and their insertion into the mitochondrial outer membrane." [PMID:40782022]
- **NARROW synonyms**: "MIM pathway", "MTCH pathway"
- **Comment**: Notes the yeast MIM complex (Mim1/Mim2) vs mammalian MTCH functional analogy
- **Ref**: PMID:40782022 (Dimogkioka & Rapaport 2025, Biol Chem — review of OMM insertases across eukaryotes)

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue analyzed, intent clear
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: Ontology validates before changes
- [x] RESEARCH: PMID:40782022 validated (Dimogkioka AR, Rapaport D, 2025, Biol Chem)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Existing terms consulted (GO:0045040, GO:7770058, GO:0140595 MIM complex)
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS: Followed existing protein insertion/import term patterns
- [x] EDITS: Used obo-checkin/checkout procedure
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: is_a GO:0045040, is_a GO:7770058
- [x] METADATA: namespace, definition with PMID, synonyms, term_tracker_item, created_by, creation_date all present
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: `make travis_build` passes (all 20 SPARQL rules pass, ELK reasoning passes)
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: PMID:40782022 confirmed valid and relevant
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED: src/ontology/go-edit.obo committed

## Test plan

- [x] `make travis_build` passes with 0 violations across all SPARQL checks
- [x] ELK reasoning completes without unsatisfiable classes
- [x] Term checked in correctly with all required metadata

content.text:
Now I understand the situation clearly:

- **PR #31803** (this branch, `run7006`): Added GO:7770059 with `is_a: GO:7770058` only
- **PR #31804** (`run7008`): Added GO:7770059 with BOTH `is_a: GO:0045040` AND `is_a: GO:7770058` — this was already merged to master

So master already has the correct version of GO:7770059 with the extra `is_a: GO:0045040`. The rebase should accept master's version. Since master already has the term, the PR branch's commit becomes a no-op after rebasing.

The simplest approach: force-push the branch to point at master, since master already contains the complete version of the change.

id: GO:7770059
name: alpha helical protein insertion into mitochondrial outer membrane
namespace: biological_process
def: "The import of alpha helical and tail-anchored proteins from the cytosol and their insertion into the mitochondrial outer membrane." [PMID:40782022]
comment: In yeast, the insertase is the mitochondrial import (MIM) complex, composed of Mim1 and Mim2. In higher eukaryotes, the functional counterpart MTCH performs a similar role despite lacking sequence and structural homology, reflecting convergent evolution.

content.text:
Master already has GO:7770059 with both `is_a` relationships (GO:0045040 and GO:7770058). Since PR #31804 was already merged with the complete version, this PR (#31803) is now redundant — its only change is already in master.

The right thing to do is update the branch to point at master (making the PR show no diff) so it can be closed cleanly.
